schema_compliance.rationale: All required fields present with correct types. Slug follows {product}--{market} convention. package_type 'project' matches product revenue_model 'project'. Positioning at 62 characters is well within the 80-char guideline. All four tiers have complete sub-structures (tier, name, included_solutions, solution_sizes, price, currency, scope). Tier labels follow the standard progression: proof-of-value, foundation, professional, enterprise.

tier_narrative_coherence.rationale: Clear qualitative capability arc: proof-of-value is a compliance assessment plus pilot (understand your gaps), foundation is a single-domain sovereign migration (secure one area), professional is multi-domain hybrid transformation with managed hyperscaler operations (orchestrate across clouds), enterprise is full-estate transformation with 24/7 managed operations and dedicated CSM (run everything). Each tier represents a fundamentally different operational maturity level, not just more features. Tier names reinforce the story: 'Compliance & Readiness Assessment' -> 'Sovereign Migration' -> 'Hybrid Cloud Transformation' -> 'Full Estate Cloud Transformation'.

solution_coverage.rationale: All four available solutions are referenced with no orphans. sovereign-cloud and cloud-migration appear in all tiers as foundational capabilities, while hybrid-cloud and managed-hyperscaler are introduced at the professional tier where the capability story demands cross-cloud orchestration. The entry tier correctly leads with sovereign-cloud (highest pain point given NIS2 October 2026 deadline) alongside cloud-migration. Solution sizes align logically with tier progression: proof_of_value -> small -> medium -> large.

pricing_architecture.rationale: Prices are monotonically increasing (40k -> 195k -> 750k -> 1,550k EUR) and each jump is justified by added capability and solutions. However, the stated bundle_savings_pct of 15% only holds for the proof-of-value tier (actual 14.9%) and foundation tier (15.2%). The professional tier delivers 20.6% savings (945k components vs 750k package) and enterprise delivers 22.5% (2,000k vs 1,550k). While the increasing discount at higher tiers is commercially sensible (price-to-value improves), the single bundle_savings_pct field misrepresents the actual tiered discount structure. The PoV-to-foundation jump of 4.9x is steep but justified by the shift from assessment to deployment.

cross_entity_consistency.rationale: Strong alignment overall. Currency EUR matches market region 'de'. All solution slugs are valid references. Tier scopes echo upstream proposition language: 'NIS2 compliance gap assessment' from sovereign-cloud DOES, 'parallel-run validation' from cloud-migration DOES, 'MCCP' from hybrid-cloud IS statement, 'SAP IS-U' from cloud-migration DOES, '24/7 managed operations' from managed-hyperscaler DOES. Minor issue: proof-of-value scope states '3-week delivery' while combining sovereign-cloud PoV (2-week delivery) and cloud-migration PoV (3-week delivery) — running both in 3 weeks implies parallelization that isn't articulated, creating a minor delivery promise inconsistency with upstream solution timelines.

top_issues.issue: bundle_savings_pct of 15% is inaccurate for professional (actual 20.6%) and enterprise (actual 22.5%) tiers — the stated percentage only holds for PoV and foundation tiers
top_issues.suggested_fix: Either use the minimum savings (15%) and note that higher tiers receive greater discounts, or replace the single bundle_savings_pct with a per-tier savings field
top_issues.root_cause_hint: packages SKILL.md — bundle savings calculation may use only the entry tier rather than validating across all tiers

top_issues.issue: Proof-of-value scope claims '3-week delivery' while combining two solution PoVs with independent timelines (sovereign-cloud 2 weeks + cloud-migration 3 weeks), implying parallel execution without stating it
top_issues.suggested_fix: Either extend to '4-week delivery' for sequential execution or explicitly state 'parallel-track delivery' in the scope
top_issues.root_cause_hint: packages SKILL.md — scope writing guidance may not instruct the agent to validate combined delivery timelines against upstream solution durations

strengths: Excellent tier narrative arc that moves from compliance assessment through sovereign migration to hybrid transformation to full estate — each tier is a qualitatively different operational maturity level, not just incremental feature addition

Strong upstream entity alignment with direct echoes of proposition DOES statements in tier scopes (NIS2, MCCP, SAP IS-U, 24/7 operations), demonstrating the package was built from rather than alongside its supporting entities

Solution grouping is strategically sound: foundational capabilities (sovereign-cloud, cloud-migration) appear in all tiers while advanced orchestration capabilities (hybrid-cloud, managed-hyperscaler) gate at the professional tier where the story demands cross-cloud integration

Positioning statement ('NIS2-ready cloud transformation for critical energy infrastructure') is concise, market-relevant, and directly tied to the regulatory urgency expressed in the sovereign-cloud proposition
overall_assessment: This package is portfolio-review ready. It demonstrates strong structural quality across all five dimensions, with a clear capability-progression narrative that would resonate in a CTO-level quarterly review. The tier design correctly sequences compliance, migration, hybrid orchestration, and full-estate operations as distinct maturity stages rather than volume bundles. The two issues identified — bundle savings percentage inconsistency and compressed PoV delivery timeline — are both correctable without restructuring the package. The package makes excellent use of upstream entity content, weaving proposition language and solution scope details into coherent tier descriptions. Overall score of 4.2 reflects a package that is commercially and architecturally sound with minor pricing metadata and delivery timeline refinements needed.
